forecast-period.text: A cold front about the southeast moves into Victoria overnight. A weak high pressure system moves across from the west on Sunday. A significant low pressure system moves over waters to the south of SA on Monday and Tuesday reaching western Bass Strait Tuesday afternoon. A another low pressure moves over waters to the southeast on Wednesday. A high pressure system follows the low moving across northern parts of SA later in the week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots offshore in the late afternoon. Winds decreasing to about 10 knots in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre later in the evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Far West Coast
forecast-period.text: Variable about 10 knots becoming northerly 15 to 25 knots in the early morning. Winds reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning westerly 30 to 35 knots in the early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
South to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres, tending west to southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres by early evening.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Westerly 25 to 35 knots decreasing to 20 to 25 knots during the evening.
3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2 to 3 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres inshore, increasing to 2 to 4 metres offshore.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre later in the evening.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the early morning then becoming northerly 15 to 25 knots in the morning. Winds reaching up to 30 knots offshore in the evening.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy.
Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning west to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots during the morning.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 2 metres, tending westerly 2.5 to 3 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
Westerly 25 to 35 knots decreasing to 20 to 25 knots during the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots.
1 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
West to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots shifting northerly 15 to 20 knots in the late morning and early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 25 knots offshore in the evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres later in the evening.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Northerly 20 to 25 knots turning west to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots in the late afternoon.
1.5 to 2 metres.
West to southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.

Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning west to northwesterly below 10 knots during the morning then tending northerly 15 to 20 knots in the early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 25 knots in the west in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre around midday, then increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres later in the evening.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning west to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots during the day.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, tending southerly 1.5 to 2 metres around midday, then tending southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres later in the evening.
Easterly below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots.
2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres in the west.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots in the afternoon.
2 to 2.5 metres.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the middle of the day then becoming northerly 15 to 25 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing below 1.5 metres around midday, then decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning northwesterly in the early afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1.5 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
East to southeasterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres west of Kangaroo Island by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots tending west to southwesterly during the day.
2.5 to 4 metres.
Southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, tending westerly 3 to 5 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots offshore in the late afternoon. Winds tending southwesterly in the late afternoon.
2 metres.
Westerly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late afternoon and evening.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the early afternoon then becoming north to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots in the late evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres by early evening.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Northerly 15 to 25 knots tending north to northeasterly 20 to 30 knots in the early morning then tending north to northwesterly in the middle of the day.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.
Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning westerly during the morning.
2 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres inshore, increasing to 2 to 3 metres offshore.
Southerly around 1 metre.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning and afternoon.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 30 knots.
2 to 2.5 metres.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late afternoon and evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Beachport during the morning. Winds turning west to northwesterly below 10 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Westerly 3 metres, tending southwesterly 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
North to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots tending north to northeasterly 20 to 30 knots before dawn then tending north to northwesterly in the late afternoon.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 2 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.
North to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning westerly during the morning.
2 to 3 metres.
Northwest to southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres south of Beachport during the afternoon or evening.
Southerly around 1 metre.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning and afternoon.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots south of Port Neill to Port Victoria in the late afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres south of Port Neill to Port Victoria.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.

West to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the middle of the day then becoming northerly 15 to 20 knots in the late afternoon. Winds reaching up to 25 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then increasing to 1 to 2 metres later in the evening.

1.5 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.

Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning westerly during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
West to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots south of Port Vincent to Brighton in the late afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
West to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the middle of the day then becoming northerly 15 to 20 knots in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre around midday.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.

Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.

Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning westerly during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots in the late afternoon.
1 to 2 metres.
Westerly 1 to 1.5 metres.

1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre around midday.
West to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre later in the evening.

Around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.

2 to 3 metres.
